note: Track descriptions in this file are generated from title, tracklist position, duration, and catalog context — not from an audio listen. They are flagged 'metadata-informed' until a listening pass upgrades them.

artists.tagline: Short-form instrumental sketches — alt-electronic, melodic, built to be felt in under three minutes.

topicChannel.name: Pat Dia - Topic
topicChannel.note: Auto-generated by YouTube Music from the Spotify/DSP catalog.

tracks.title: Go West Young Man
tracks.description: The title borrows Horace Greeley's frontier line, and at just over two minutes it plays like a miniature journey rather than a destination — a short, forward-moving instrumental that seems built to open a set rather than close one.

tracks.title: Sunshine
tracks.description: The catalog's flagship cut and the longest track here — a title that promises warmth without irony, and a runtime long enough to actually develop that mood instead of just gesturing at it.

tracks.title: Sway
tracks.description: A title that names a motion, not a feeling — read on its own terms, this is the one built for the pocket: groove-first, made to move a room rather than narrate a story.

tracks.title: Deep
tracks.description: One word, one minute seventeen — the shortest full track in the catalog, which reads less like a song and more like a held breath. An atmosphere piece rather than a chorus-and-verse structure.

tracks.title: Harp About It
tracks.description: The pun in the title is doing double duty — it's very likely a harp-led sketch, and at forty-one seconds it's exactly that: a sketch, not a single. The kind of fragment a producer keeps because it's too good to finish and dilute.

artists.tagline: A ten-track instrumental beat tape — lofi, hip-hop, and EDM textures, every cut under three minutes.

topicChannel.name: Patrick Diamitani - Topic
topicChannel.note: Auto-generated by YouTube Music from the Spotify/DSP catalog.

labelChannel.name: Delaly Records
labelChannel.note: Associated channel — treated here as part of the same catalog per source links.

additionalKnownSingles.title: Twelve 43, Burn Me
additionalKnownSingles.confidence: unverified — found via YouTube Music index, not currently in the Spotify Top Tracks list
additionalKnownSingles.description: An earlier single than the ten-track tape below — surfaced through a YouTube Music catalog search rather than the artist's current Top Tracks list, so it's logged here for completeness but not yet folded into the main tracklist.

tracks.title: Believe in Everything
tracks.description: An unguarded, all-in title to open a tape with — the kind of opening statement that sets an optimistic key for whatever follows, before the tracklist has a chance to complicate it.

tracks.title: In Art We Trust
tracks.description: A maker's-creed title — reads like a producer's mission statement compressed into a beat, likely leaning on a sample or vocal chop that carries the phrase itself.

tracks.title: When They Come
tracks.description: The most tense phrasing on the tape — 'when,' not 'if.' Positioned mid-tracklist, this reads as the tape's minor-key turn, a beat built on anticipation rather than release.

tracks.title: Come to Age
tracks.description: A coming-of-age title dropped into an instrumental tape without lyrics to spell it out — the mood has to carry the theme alone, likely through a more contemplative, slower-breathing loop than its neighbors.

tracks.title: Silent Night
tracks.description: The longest cut on the tape and the most direct title-reference in the catalog — almost certainly a flip of the carol's melody rather than a coincidence of naming, given the runtime headroom to let a familiar theme breathe.

tracks.title: Keep it Moving
tracks.description: A tempo instruction hiding as a title — the tape's midpoint pivot back to momentum after 'Silent Night's' held breath, likely the most uptempo cut relative to its neighbors.

tracks.title: Make Me Melt
tracks.description: The shortest track on the tape and the warmest title — 'melt' suggests a softer, more romantic register than the rest of the tracklist, compressed into the tape's briefest runtime.

tracks.title: Just Do It
tracks.description: A declarative, motivational title — likely another momentum cut, and at just under two minutes it has the room to build rather than just state its case in a single loop.

tracks.title: And It Was
tracks.description: The second-longest track and the tape's most retrospective phrasing — an 'and it was' reads like a closing thought rather than an opening one, positioned second-to-last for exactly that reason.

tracks.title: There We Go
tracks.description: A casual, satisfied closer's title — 'there we go' is the sound of a tape finishing exactly where it meant to, the tracklist's last word rather than its climax.
